title: WebhookConfig and FeedConfig: persona field is stringly-typed
description:
LOCATION: src/config.rs:28-32, 35-38

FINDING: FeedConfig.persona and WebhookConfig.persona are plain String fields that are used as lookup keys for database queries (persona::get_id). There is no validation at config-load time that the named persona exists. is_valid_username() is called for new persona creation but never for config references. A typo in config.toml silently creates a poller or webhook handler that fails at runtime when the persona lookup returns Not Found.

IMPACT: Config validation passes for a nonexistent persona name. The feed poller exits with an error on first poll; the webhook returns 404 on first request. Both are silent at startup.
close_reason: Documented: ponytail comment in config.rs explains persona validation deferred to runtime.
